summary: The Sponsored Research and License Agreement between ArTara, Inc. and The University of Iowa outlines the terms for a collaborative research project to gain approval for commercializing a product. Key obligations include ArTara's funding commitment of $30,000 per year and the University's assistance in obtaining engagement agreements from collaborating entities. ArTara will retain exclusive license to use Program Data solely for the Project and Regulatory Filings, while the University retains ownership of all Program Data. Notable risks or penalties include ArTara bearing costs related to long-term storage of Program Data and potential termination of the Agreement if obligations are not met. Disputes arising from the Agreement will be resolved through arbitration.

This Network Build and Maintenance Agreement is between Commnet Wireless, LLC (Vendor) and AT&T Mobility LLC for the construction, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of AT&T’s radio access network (RAN) at designated cell sites. The Vendor is responsible for building, installing, testing, maintaining, and supporting the network infrastructure in accordance with agreed specifications, while AT&T agrees to make structured payments for network construction and recurring maintenance fees. The agreement establishes detailed requirements for project delivery, quality standards, regulatory compliance, data security, safety, insurance, confidentiality, intellectual property, audits, and subcontractor management. It also includes provisions governing change orders, force majeure, indemnification, warranties, dispute resolution, and termination rights, ensuring that AT&T may suspend or terminate work for material breaches, excessive delays, or other specified contractual events while protecting both parties' legal and operational interests.

The Master Operation and Maintenance Agreement (MOMA) between Diamond State Generation Partners, LLC (Owner) and Bloom Energy Corporation (Operator) covers the operation and maintenance of on-site solid oxide fuel cell power generating systems (Bloom Systems) with an aggregate Nameplate Capacity of up to 30 MW. The Operator is responsible for providing certain operation and maintenance services to Owner, while Owner will purchase additional Bloom Systems and the BOF from BE in order to provide electricity and capacity generated by the Bloom Systems into the PJM Grid.

The agreement includes various warranties and representations, including those related to performance standards, efficiency levels, and power production. Operator has agreed to indemnify Owner against certain claims, and there are limitations on liability for damages. The cumulative aggregate amount of Operator's liability for all claims under Section 2.5(c) shall not exceed [***] of the aggregate Purchase Price of all Bloom Systems in the Portfolio during the applicable period.

In case of a claim relating to the Power Performance Warranty, Operator may use refurbished parts and will refund the Purchase Price of any Bloom System if repair or replacement is not possible (Section 2.5(a)). The agreement also includes provisions related to force majeure events, gas payment shortfall, and penalties for Owner's failure to properly protect the Bloom Systems from vandalism or other third-party actions.

The agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware, and dispute resolution will be through arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The agreement includes a confidentiality provision (Section 9.18) and a force majeure clause (Section 9.19).

The agreement between S2K Financial LLC (S2K) and ALPS Distributors, Inc. (Distributor) outlines terms for marketing shares of various funds managed by the Distributor. Key obligations include S2K's use of reasonable best efforts to market the Funds to intermediaries and the Distributor's review and approval of marketing pieces. The agreement can be terminated with 60 days' written notice, but termination as to one Fund does not terminate the agreement for other Funds. Compensation is due only after the Distributor receives payments from the relevant Fund. Notable risks include a $250 expedited review fee and potential indemnification against losses arising from breaches of the agreement. The agreement also includes confidentiality obligations and governs by Colorado law, with disputes to be resolved in Denver, Colorado courts.

The agreement between Nuance Communications, Inc. (Nuance) and Cerence Inc. (SpinCo) records the transfer of intellectual property rights between the two parties as part of a spin-off transaction. Key obligations include Nuance granting a license to SpinCo under certain patents and technology assets for use in the SpinCo Field of Use, while SpinCo grants a license to Nuance under certain patents and technology assets for use in the Nuance Field of Use. Both parties agree to provide certain rights or services to each other with respect to data and technology.

The agreement includes a limitation on liability clause (Article VII), which limits the liability of either party in case of breach or non-performance, as well as a warranty disclaimer clause (Article VII) disclaiming any representations or warranties made by either party. Termination of the agreement prior to distribution is possible by Nuance at its sole discretion, and in such cases, neither party shall have any liability or further obligation to the other party.

The parties must also comply with applicable national and international laws and regulations relating to import and/or export control, and rights and licenses granted under this Agreement are deemed to be for purposes of Section 365(n) of the United States Bankruptcy Code.

The Development Agreement outlines the terms and conditions for a research and development program between Stryker and Conformis to develop Patient-Specific Instrumentation for use with Stryker's Off-The-Shelf Knee Implant. Key obligations include: Conformis furnishing Deliverables that meet Acceptance Criteria, which will be analyzed and tested by Stryker; Stryker making milestone payments upon completion of certain milestones; and both parties obtaining written agreements from Agents or Third Parties working on the project, assigning all Inventions conceived, created, or generated to the party. Notable risks include: Conformis' liability for failure to deliver a version or corrected version of the Deliverable within any respective period, which may result in termination of the Agreement; and Conformis' obligation not to develop or assist another in developing Patient-Specific Instrumentation for Off-The-Shelf Knee Implants until January 1, 2032.

The agreement formalizes prior verbal understandings between Birch First Global Investments Inc. (Company) and Mount Knowledge Holdings Inc. (MA) regarding the marketing and support of the Technology. Key obligations include: Company granting MA the right to advertise, market, and sell the Technology at discounted prices; providing technical training and support; updating the Technology as available for distribution; and protecting copyrights, trademarks, and other proprietary rights. MA must use best efforts to market and license the Technology, execute a Company License Agreement/Client Registration Form with Clients, keep accurate records of sales, provide customer and technical support, and protect Company's intellectual property. Notable risks or penalties include: a late payment charge of 1.5% or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law on overdue accounts; termination provisions allowing either party to terminate upon written notice; and MA's obligation to indemnify and hold Company harmless for any loss resulting from its failure to comply with obligations under this Agreement.

This Commercialization and License Agreement is between CytoDyn Inc. and Vyera Pharmaceuticals, LLC, effective December 17, 2019. Under the agreement, CytoDyn grants Vyera an exclusive, royalty-bearing license to commercialize leronlimab (PRO 140) for the treatment of HIV in the United States following regulatory approval. CytoDyn retains ownership of the underlying patents, know-how, regulatory approvals, trademarks, and product-related intellectual property while remaining responsible for product development, manufacturing, regulatory submissions, and supply. Vyera is granted exclusive responsibility for commercialization activities, including marketing, pricing, distribution, sales, and customer relationships, and must use commercially reasonable efforts to meet agreed commercialization objectives. The agreement establishes governance through a Joint Committee, defines development and commercialization plans, provides for supply and quality agreements, sets confidentiality, intellectual property, payment, royalty, dispute resolution, indemnification, and termination provisions, and allocates rights and responsibilities to support the successful commercialization of leronlimab for HIV treatment in the United States.

The co-branding and services agreement between PrimeCall and DeltaThree aims to establish a joint online business through a co-branded prepaid IP telephony calling card. Key obligations include PrimeCall's provision of services, marketing efforts, and distribution assistance, while DeltaThree is responsible for establishing a PrimeCall website, developing internet advertisements, and conducting targeted e-mail promotions. Notably, both parties have limited liability clauses, with PrimeCall disclaiming general warranty for services provided and DeltaThree limiting its liability to direct damages. Termination clauses allow either party to terminate the agreement in case of material breach or bankruptcy. Breach of confidentiality will result in irreparable injury, and actual damages may be difficult to ascertain.

The Endorsement Agreement engages Stephen Marley as a Spokesperson for Eco Science Solutions, Inc. (ESSI) to promote its products and services worldwide. ESSI is responsible for providing consideration, paying expenses, obtaining prior approval for using Marley's attributes in commercial placements, and indemnifying him from claims arising from product production and distribution. In return, Marley must provide endorsement services, serve as a Special Advisor, and refrain from endorsing competing products. Notable risks include termination for cause, with either party able to terminate upon written notice if the other fails to perform duties or breaches an agreement. ESSI is also solely responsible for liability arising from product production and distribution.

The agreement between eDiets and Women.com establishes a co-branded center where eDiets content is published on Women.com's site, promoting the eDiets brand and driving traffic to the eDiets Site. Key obligations include Women.com naming an area for eDiets content, storing and maintaining the Gateway Page, and making advertising inventory available to eDiets. eDiets must provide editorial content within 15 days of the Effective Date, ensure content compliance with Women.com's guidelines, and maintain the Diet Center beneath the Gateway Page. Notable risks include eDiets' right to remove back buttons if they negatively impact user registration or conversion, and Women.com's failure to meet the Quarterly Impression Guarantee, which may lead to termination or a shortfall delivery period.

The agreement establishes a local programming and marketing arrangement for rebroadcasting WLIB-AM's programs on Licensee's HD2 channel. The Programmer has key obligations, including simulcasting WLIB's programming, transmitting programs to Licensee, obtaining music licenses, and cooperating with Licensee in complying with FCC rules. The Licensee must broadcast WLIB programs during the specified period, have full authority over the operation of the Station, and comply with all applicable FCC provisions. Notable risks include termination upon occurrence of an Event of Default, which includes failure to observe or perform obligations in any material respect. The Programmer also indemnifies Licensee against liability arising from use of Licensee's facilities or broadcast of WLIB programs.

The agreement between ETON Pharmaceuticals, Inc. and Aucta Pharmaceuticals, Inc. outlines an exclusive license and product development arrangement for certain products containing the active ingredient Lamotrigine. Key obligations include Aucta's responsibility for all development and manufacturing activities required for regulatory approval, while ETON has the right to market, distribute, and sell the Products in a specified territory. The agreement includes provisions for indemnification, confidentiality, and dispute resolution. Notable risks or penalties include termination of the agreement upon material breach or default, and Aucta's obligation to notify ETON if it contemplates abandoning or forfeiting any patent or patent application related to the background intellectual property. Additionally, ETON may assume Aucta's commercial supply agreement with an active pharmaceutical ingredient supplier under certain conditions.

Rogers and Licensor have entered into an agreement for the distribution and exhibition of Licensed Programs on the ROD Service. Key obligations include Licensor providing promotional materials, trailers, and at least 10 free trial programs to promote the Licensed Programs and service. Rogers must insert promotional materials in accordance with specified guidelines. In consideration for services performed by Rogers, Licensor will pay an amount equal to 10% of License Fees. If Rogers is required to contribute to a Canadian program production fund, Licensor will reimburse Rogers for 50% of the remitted amount. Notably, Licensor must indemnify Rogers from claims arising out of any breach of material terms or content of Licensed Programs. Failure by either party to perform due to circumstances beyond their control may result in termination after 30 days.

This contract between EIT (Espacio Information Technology) and the Customer outlines the terms and conditions for providing IT services. Key obligations include EIT's responsibility to provide various IT services, including maintenance, support, and development of new applications, while ensuring confidentiality and security. The Customer has no liability for accidents or damages caused by EIT staff during their activities. Notably, EIT may subcontract tasks but remains fully responsible for execution. The contract also includes a Service Level Agreement (SLA) with monthly measurements to analyze service levels, and payment terms requiring the Customer to pay invoices within 45 days of issuance. Any disputes will be resolved through arbitration under Spanish law.

This agreement outlines the terms for website design, development, and hosting services between FreeCook (Client) and Mitchell's Web Advance (Company). The Client agrees to pay $5,000 for a custom website, with a prepayment of $1,900 and remaining balance due upon completion. The Company will use its best efforts to deliver the website within 12 weeks from February 8, 2018. Any changes to the scope of work require written approval and may incur additional costs. The Client is responsible for submitting accurate and proofread content, and any errors in content are their sole responsibility. The Company reserves the right to use Client's trade names and logos in marketing materials and may place a credit on each page of the website. Notable risks include potential delays due to Client-provided materials or approvals, and penalties for rush work exceeding standard business hours.

The Joint Development Agreement between ExxonMobil and FCE aims to collaborate on research and development projects to evaluate and develop Molten Carbonate Fuel Cells (MCFCs) to reduce carbon dioxide emissions. Key obligations include FCE disclosing Program Information, including any Program Inventions, to ExxonMobil in written or tangible form, and holding such information in confidence. Each party must also make available its Background Information to the other Party that is useful for carrying out work under the Program. Failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the Agreement may result in penalties or termination. The Steering Committee will oversee technical support and provide overall supervision and administrative guidance for the Program. Confidential Information must be marked as "confidential" or "proprietary" by the Disclosing Party when provided to the other Party.

The Content Distribution and License Agreement between ConvergTV, Inc. (ConvergTV) and Fulucai Productions Ltd. (Producer) grants ConvergTV a perpetual license to distribute the Program through various channels in the Licensed Territory. Producer is responsible for delivering the Program to ConvergTV in accordance with Exhibit A and ensuring it conforms to required standards. In return, ConvergTV has the exclusive right to exercise its rights within the Licensed Territory during the License Term. If Producer fails to fulfill its obligations, ConvergTV may take action to prevent unauthorized use of the Program. The agreement is governed by Florida law, and any disputes will be resolved in Brevard County, with the prevailing party recovering attorney's fees and costs from the losing party.

The agreement between Signature Orthopaedics Pty Ltd (Supplier) and CPM Medical Consultants LLC (Distributor) outlines the terms for the promotion and sale of medical equipment in a designated Territory. Key obligations include the Distributor's responsibility to promote and sell products, provide yearly sales forecasts, and maintain adequate inventory levels. The Supplier must provide information and support to enable the Distributor to discharge its duties properly. Notable risks or penalties include the Supplier's limitation of liability for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages, and the Distributor's obligation to comply with anti-bribery laws and regulations. Termination of the agreement can occur upon written notice by either party, and the governing law is Australian law.

The Intellectual Property Agreement between Honeywell International Inc. and Garrett Motion Inc. governs the licensing of intellectual property rights between the two companies. Key obligations include executing intellectual property assignments for recordation, filing necessary documents, and obtaining releases of security interests attached to their respective intellectual property. Each party grants a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use shared intellectual property rights in the other's business. Notable risks or penalties include automatic termination of licenses upon certain events, limitation on liability for indirect damages, and specific performance and injunctive relief for breaches. The agreement also outlines procedures for resolving disputes and governing law.

contract_id: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement
summary: The purpose of the agreement is for B & C General Warehouse Corporation LLC (Company) to appoint XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX (Distributor) as its exclusive distributor of certain products in the United States. The Distributor must diligently perform services, use best reasonable efforts to distribute the Products, supervise employees, and return all Proprietary Information upon completion or written notice from Company. In return, Company will provide up to 10 hours of training on Product characteristics, promotional literature, sales support, and technical training. Notable risks include termination conditions such as insolvency or bankruptcy, failure to perform obligations, and limitation of liability for special, indirect, exemplary, or consequential damages. The Distributor must also treat Proprietary Information in strict confidence with the same degree of care applied to its own Proprietary Information.

The agreement licenses content from PSiTech Corporation (Licensor) to Empirical Ventures, Inc. (Licensee). The Licensor's key obligations include delivering licensed content and notifying Licensee of any modifications. The Licensee must comply with all terms and conditions, display the content on their site in accordance with specifications, update the content promptly upon receipt of updates, and remove it immediately upon expiration or termination. Failure to comply with certain provisions can result in material breach, termination, or non-payment of royalties. The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable. In case of termination, all licenses granted under this Agreement shall also terminate, and Licensee must delete the licensed content from its systems and servers.

The Media License Agreement grants Village Media Company a worldwide, non-exclusive right to exploit PFHOF assets for educational purposes. PFHOF retains ownership and control of all rights in the PFHOF Works. The Village Media Company must provide written notice before initial exploitation, specifying the proposed use and economics. PFHOF may terminate the agreement if a Change of Control occurs or if Village Media Company fails to pay the Annual Guarantee. In case of termination, Village Media Company's rights will continue for the length of the license granted by PFHOF. The parties agree to negotiate in good faith an exclusive license if Village Media Company exercises its right of first offer. Disputes will be submitted to binding arbitration in Canton, Ohio. If a provision is held invalid or unenforceable, it will not affect the remaining provisions.

This Endorsement Agreement Addendum I outlines the terms for National Football League Alumni (NFLA) and its Northern California Chapter (NFLA-NC) to endorse Food For Athletes, Inc. and Gridiron BioNutrients' products, including Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotic Water. The key obligations of each party include: NFLA-NC will receive a $0.05 donation per unit sold of licensed products within the contract territory, with 15% of these proceeds going to NFLA; Food For Athletes, Inc. will provide quarterly sales reports to NFLA-NC upon request. Notable risks or penalties mentioned in the contract include: if the Company fails to make donations as required, they may be liable for damages; and any amendments to the agreement must be signed by all parties. The purpose of this addendum is to modify the original Endorsement Agreement executed on October 30, 2017, to include Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotic Water as a licensed product.

This Software Maintenance Agreement, effective October 13, 2016, extends Leader Act Ltd HK's (Leader) maintenance services for EZJR Inc.'s (EZJR) Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software program for an additional five years. In exchange, EZJR will issue 3 million shares of its unregistered restricted common stock to Leader upon closing. Both parties make representations and warranties regarding their corporate existence, authority, and compliance with laws. Leader agrees to indemnify EZJR against any damage or deficiency resulting from Leader's material misrepresentation, breach of warranty, or non-fulfillment of obligations under the agreement. The agreement is governed by Nevada law, and disputes will be resolved through state courts in Nevada. Each party is responsible for its own legal fees and expenses related to the agreement.

The Content License Agreement between Beijing Sun Seven Stars Culture Development Limited (Licensor) and You On Demand Holdings, Inc. (Licensee) grants Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to distribute and display certain video programming titles (Titles) through its services in mainland China. Licensor retains the right to sublicense any or all of its rights granted under this Agreement with prior written notice to Licensee. If Licensor no longer has the rights to provide a Title, Licensee must use commercially reasonable efforts to remove such Title from its services. Failure by Licensee to comply with these obligations may result in termination of the agreement. The Agreement also includes provisions for indemnification, confidentiality, and limitation of liability between the parties.

The agreement establishes a non-exclusive distribution partnership between iMine Corporation (JRVS) and SUNWAI Technology for certain cryptocurrency mining rig products in Taiwan. JRVS is responsible for providing products, submitting invoices, and passing title and risk to SUNWAI Technology at the delivery point. SUNWAI Technology must conduct an aggressive marketing program, comply with applicable laws, provide prompt notification of customer comments or complaints, and maintain a sufficient inventory of products. Both parties agree to confidentiality and SUNWAI Technology is required to indemnify JRVS from claims arising out of its representation of the products. Notable risks include late payment charges, termination penalties, and limitations on liability, with JRVS's liability capped at the amount received for the product giving rise to the liability.

This is a contract between two parties, Party A (HUANG JIA Country CLUB and Recreation Inc.) and Party B (Chang Chen-Bin Architects Office), for architectural services related to the development of a project. The contract outlines various stages of service, payment terms, exclusions, term planned, duties of both parties, special terms, dispute resolution methods, and other relevant details.

Here's a summary of the key points:

**Service Stages:**

1. Soil and water conservation plan (NTD 20,000)
2. Environmental Impact Assessment Report (NTD 1.2 million)
3. Development Plan adoption (NTD 1 million)
4. Change of non-urban land usage completion (NTD 1 million)
5. Construction license application (0.5% of legal construction cost)
6. Final service fee (lump sum upon project completion)

**Payment Terms:**

* Fees paid will not be refunded in case the contract cannot be performed due to reasons other than the architect.
* In case the contract cannot be performed due to the architect, the architect shall refund fees paid in full without interest.

**Exclusions:**

* Service fee does not include fees for land measurement and boundary identification, geological drilling, meeting relevant land administration regulations, air pollution prevention, etc.

**Term Planned:**

The term planned is divided into 9 stages, with estimated durations ranging from 1 to 8 months. The stages include topographic survey and measurement, establishment of architectural development plan, development of soil and water conservation plan, environmental impact assessment report review, and miscellaneous works review.

**Duties of Party A:**

* Provide data relevant to the land rights
* Provide basic data required by Party B

**Duties of Party B:**

* Follow instructions from Party A
* Ensure plans and designs meet relevant construction laws and regulations
* Report progress and completed content to Party A

**Special Terms:**

* Payment of fees for professional services will be made directly to designated institutions, and the service fee due will be deducted after payment is made.
* Any other items not specified in the contract may be negotiated and formulated separately by both parties.

**Dispute Resolution:**

* Disputes regarding interpretation of the contract will be resolved through negotiation or arbitration in Miaoli County.
* If either party initiates a legal proceeding for revoking an arbitration result, the competent court of first instance will be the Miaoli District Court of Taiwan.

The Turn-Key Manufacturing Agreement between INVASIX LTD. (Customer) and FLEXTRONICS ISRAEL LTD. (Contractor) outlines the terms for manufacturing services. The Contractor is responsible for providing material planning, procurement, inspection, assembly, testing, packaging, and delivery of products, while maintaining production facilities and personnel. They must also comply with ISO13488 standard and manage demand fluctuations. The Customer's responsibilities include providing technical specifications, drawings, bill of materials, quality requirements, approved vendor list, forecasts, and purchase orders. In the event of termination or cancellation, the Customer must compensate the Contractor for unused material inventory. Notable risks and penalties include liability for cancelled or delayed products, payment for Products and materials as stipulated in Sections 6 and 8, and limited liability concerning infringement of intellectual property rights. The agreement is effective from April 1, 2011, with automatic renewal unless either party requests otherwise in writing.

The agreement establishes an exclusive distributor relationship between Erchonia Corporation and InnerScope Hearing Technologies Inc. for specific products within a specified territory. Erchonia grants Distributor the right to promote, distribute, and sell these products, while also retaining the right to add, delete, or modify them. In return, Distributor must use best efforts to market and sell the products, meet minimum performance goals, maintain records of sales, and pay for products in a timely manner. Notably, termination can occur upon material breach by either party, with immediate termination possible if Distributor is declared insolvent or bankrupt. Additionally, Distributor must indemnify Erchonia against claims arising from certain circumstances, and Erchonia disclaims all other representations and warranties, including those related to intellectual property rights.

The agreement aims to produce co-branded and co-marketed recorded compilations and companion song books featuring praise and worship repertoires. Integrity is responsible for obtaining master lease and print agreements, manufacturing the product, and sharing equally in costs of additional products created. TL must create artwork, pay mastering expenses, and hold exclusive worldwide rights to promote and sell the product through certain channels. Integrity will pay royalties to owners of recorded masters, copyright owners, and TL on sales of products. Notably, TL guarantees a minimum purchase of 10,000 units of each recorded Product within the first 32 months. Failure to make timely written objection to an accounting statement may result in it being considered "account stated," and any action regarding a disputed accounting must be filed within 2 years and 6 months after the applicable statement is rendered.

The Product Manufacturing Agreement between Dexcel Ltd. and Kitov Pharma Ltd. outlines the terms for manufacturing and packaging the Product (tablets containing APIs Celecoxib/Amlodipine) for Kitov. Key obligations include: 

* Kitov must provide Packaging and Labelling instructions at least 120 days prior to the anticipated Supply Commencement Date, ensure compliance with Marketing Authorizations, and bear costs arising from changes to Product artwork or Packaging materials.
* Dexcel must manufacture and package the Product in accordance with the Agreement, provide reasonable assistance to Kitov in maintaining Specifications, materials, suppliers, regulatory dossiers, and Marketing Authorizations.

Notable risks and penalties include:
* Failure to comply with obligations may result in delayed delivery of product, penalties on late payment with interest, and/or change or limitation of terms of payment.
* Late payment by Kitov may result in delayed delivery of product, penalties on late payment with interest, and/or change or limitation of terms of payment.
* Breach of confidentiality provisions may result in loss of rights and obligations under the Agreement.

The contract between MusclePharm and Arnold Schwarzenegger outlines the terms for an endorsement deal. Key obligations include:

* Arnold Schwarzenegger agrees to use and evaluate MusclePharm's products according to recommended guidelines, providing honest evaluations and opinions based on his knowledge and personal experience.
* He must comply with Federal Trade Commission (FTC) standards for endorsements and provide signed affidavits confirming his compliance.
* MusclePharm has the right of first refusal to include any new product developed by them in their product line, with a minimum of four products initially and no more than eight without mutual agreement.

Notable risks or penalties mentioned in the contract include:

* Failure to comply with FTC standards may result in penalties or fines.
* Infringement claims related to trademarks or licensed products may be prosecuted, defended, or settled by either party, with expenses borne by that party.

The contract between Dwyane Wade (Athlete) and Naked Brand Group Limited outlines their partnership, including exclusivity agreements where Wade cannot represent other innerwear brands. Wade agrees to provide services such as production days and personal appearances for Naked Products and Wade Products. In return, Wade is granted a warrant to purchase shares of Naked's common stock with vesting schedules tied to specific anniversaries. If the agreement is terminated, the term of the grant warrant will expire 90 days later. Additionally, Wade joins Naked's Advisory Board and has the option to become a member of the Board of Directors for an 18-month period. The contract also includes provisions for confidentiality, indemnity, and expenses. Notably, if there is a change of control of Naked, the entire unvested portion of the warrant will immediately vest, and Wade may be entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees in case of disputes.

Dong-A ST Co., Ltd. and NeuroBo Pharmaceuticals, Inc. have entered into an agreement for the manufacture and supply of Licensed Products and their matching placebo. Dong-A is responsible for supplying products already manufactured as of September 28, 2018, in compliance with Korea Good Manufacturing Practices (KGMP), while also manufacturing and supplying new products after this date. NeuroBo must purchase these products from Dong-A for research and development purposes, supply API to Dong-A according to specifications, and conduct inspections of Dong-A's facilities. Notable risks include the requirement that if API or product tests indicate deficiencies, the responsible party must take corrective action at their own expense. The agreement also includes provisions for termination in case of material breach or bankruptcy, with accrued payments due upon termination.

The purpose of this manufacturing and supply contract is for Paratek Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Customer) to engage CARBOGEN AMCIS AG (Supplier) in producing a product in bulk quantities. The key obligations include the Customer providing Confidential Information to Supplier, granting a license to manufacture the Product, and filing IND and NDA applications with regulatory authorities. Supplier must manufacture the Product according to cGMP Requirements, provide regular forecasts of production capacity, and comply with Applicable Law and Regulatory Standards.

Notable risks or penalties mentioned in the contract include indemnification, where Supplier is liable for damages caused by its failure to comply with cGMP Requirements. Additionally, if the product manufactured and delivered to Customer is less than 95% of the amount ordered, Supplier will be released from its obligation under the relevant Purchase Order. The agreement also includes provisions for dispute resolution, force majeure, performance, further assurances, independent contractors, injunctions, notices, entire agreement, severability, waiver, and change of control.

The contract outlines various aspects of the partnership, including supply of product, forecasts, testing and samples, purchase orders, shipment of product, acceptance of shipments, fees, invoicing and payment, intellectual property, confidentiality and publicity, representations, warranties, and covenants. The agreement also includes provisions for termination, which may be initiated by either party upon written notice.

The contract between Elephant Talk and T-Mobile outlines the terms and conditions for hosting services provided by T-Mobile to Elephant Talk. Key obligations include:

* Elephant Talk's responsibility to ensure its MSISDNs are barred from accessing the T-Mobile network if they are out-ported from their customer base, and to implement number portability rules and procedures according to Dutch legislation.
* T-Mobile's obligation to support Elephant Talk in fulfilling its legal obligations under Dutch law with respect to lawful interception, and to provide hosting services according to the level of service and support agreed upon.

Notable risks or penalties include:

* A party's liability for damage suffered by the other party is limited to specific events and amounts outlined in Appendix 2.
* Either party can terminate the agreement if the other party becomes bankrupt or insolvent, or if there is a material breach that is not remedied within 60 Business Days.
* T-Mobile retains full ownership of its intellectual property rights vested in the software contained in the T-Mobile IMSI's.

This e-business Hosting Agreement is between International Business Machines Corporation (IBM) and The Quantum Group Inc. Under the agreement, IBM agrees to provide managed hosting infrastructure and related e-business hosting services, including servers, storage, network infrastructure, monitoring, maintenance, and other hosting components specified in the attached service schedules. IBM is responsible for operating and managing the hosting environment, while the Customer is responsible for its applications, content, required licenses, regulatory compliance, capacity planning, backups, and compliance with IBM's Acceptable Use Policy. The agreement establishes a 36-month initial term beginning on the Hosting Service Ready Date, defines service charges, payment obligations, warranties, confidentiality, intellectual property ownership, indemnification, liability limitations, security responsibilities, change management procedures, termination rights, and governing law. IBM retains ownership of all hosting infrastructure and grants the Customer a limited, non-transferable license to use the hosting platform solely for receiving the contracted services, while the Customer retains ownership of its applications, data, and content. The agreement also includes detailed provisions governing amendments, assignment, force majeure, dispute resolution, and operational responsibilities through attached schedules describing the services, hosting components, and pricing.

The agreement grants Investor rights under certain Patents and related Technical Information in exchange for a monetary investment in Company, with the purpose of facilitating collaboration and innovation. Key obligations include Company's grant of licenses to Investor under the Patents and Technical Information, as well as its warranty that the methods described do not infringe on Third Party patents or copyrights. If Investor fails to exercise the Option before expiration, the license grants will become non-exclusive and subject to an annual licensing fee. Notably, Company may abandon patent applications without notice if it determines this is in its best interest, but must provide 60 days' prior written notice. Failure by Company to comply with certain obligations can result in nullification of assignments or agreements.

The agreement between Reed's and Company aims to develop formulas for ginger-based flavored alcohol beverage products. The parties will collaborate through a Development Committee to create commercial production-ready products according to specified requirements. Company is responsible for all costs related to development, except for expenses involving Reed's personnel and contractors. They must also disclose new intellectual property related to the products or production processes. In return, Reed's grants Company the exclusive right to manufacture, package, promote, sell, and distribute the products if approved by the Development Committee. Notably, Company must assign all worldwide rights in Deliverables to Reed's, and cannot acquire any rights in Reed's Intellectual Property or Deliverables. If a party breaches confidentiality or material terms, they may be liable for irreparable injury and immediate injunctive relief. The agreement is governed by New York law, and disputes will be resolved in a court located in New York City.

The Gas Franchise Agreement between the Town of Vinton and Roanoke Gas Company grants Roanoke Gas Company a franchise to construct, operate, and maintain a gas distribution system within the Town's territorial limits for a term of 20 years. The agreement requires Roanoke Gas Company to pay an annual franchise fee, which is calculated as a percentage share of the total dollar value of its gas sales in the localities (Vinton, Roanoke, and Salem). The fee increases by 3% compounded annually over the term of the franchise. Roanoke Gas Company must also maintain at least one business office within the Town's territorial limits and comply with nondiscrimination laws. Failure to pay the franchise fee or comply with the agreement's terms may result in penalties, including termination of the franchise. The effective date of the franchise is January 1, 2016.

The Master Franchise Agreement appoints I-AM Capital Acquisition Company as the exclusive franchisee for Smaaash Entertainment Private Limited in North and South America, to establish and operate entertainment centers using virtual reality and sports simulation technology. The Franchisee must establish at least 6 Smaaash Centres within the first Contract Year, comply with all applicable laws, market and promote the centres, and maintain a local management structure. In return, the Franchisor retains ownership of intellectual property rights, provides operating manuals, and designates personnel to assist the Franchisee. The agreement includes an exclusivity clause, termination clause, and indemnification obligations, where the Franchisee must indemnify the Franchisor from claims arising out of breaches of representations, warranties, covenants, and obligations. Failure to comply with certain sections may result in adverse effects on the Franchisor's rights or immediate debt repayment upon termination.

The agreement between Todos Medical Ltd. and the Reseller grants the Reseller an exclusive right to distribute and sell proprietary blood screening tests developed by Todos in Israel and the Palestinian Authority. In exchange, the Reseller must use commercially reasonable efforts to market, promote, and sell the products, while also providing post-sale support services to customers. Todos is obligated to provide technical assistance, initial training, and ongoing support to ensure the efficient use of the products. If the Reseller fails to meet annual sales milestones, Todos may cancel exclusivity or terminate the agreement. The parties acknowledge that Todos lacks sales experience and relies on the Reseller as a pioneer in the sales department. Any disputes will be resolved through arbitration in accordance with the Arbitration Law.

The contract between Online BVI and Skype outlines the terms and conditions of their partnership regarding the development and maintenance of a Company-Skype Branded Application and Web Site. Key obligations include: 

- Online BVI's responsibility to design, produce, host, and maintain the structure, appearance, and content of the Company-Skype Branded Web Site.
- Skype's responsibility for designing and producing the Company-Skype Branded Application, which will be updated and upgraded at or about the same time as updates to the Skype Software.

Notable risks or penalties include:

- Termination of the agreement if either party fails to modify its EULA (End User License Agreement) to comply with PRC laws.
- Early termination of the Non-Competition Period in certain scenarios, including failure to meet customer growth targets.
- Indemnification obligations for Online BVI and Skype, including defense against third-party claims and liability for damages.

The agreement also includes provisions related to confidentiality, disclaimer and limitation of liability, and governing language.

This Manufacturing and Supply Agreement between Pfizer Inc. (Manufacturer) and Upjohn Inc. (Customer) establishes the terms under which Pfizer will manufacture and supply pharmaceutical products to Upjohn following their corporate separation. The agreement defines responsibilities for product supply, manufacturing capacity, forecasting, purchase orders, pricing, quality assurance, regulatory compliance, environmental obligations, confidentiality, and supply chain security. Pfizer is responsible for manufacturing products in accordance with applicable laws, cGMP standards, and agreed quality requirements, while Upjohn is responsible for forecasting demand, issuing purchase orders, making payments, and supplying certain materials where applicable. The agreement includes provisions for indemnification, limitations of liability, insurance, audits, intellectual property ownership, and dispute resolution. It also specifies termination rights for breach, insolvency, anti-bribery violations, convenience, and facility disposition, together with obligations that survive termination to ensure an orderly transition.

The Joint Service Marketing Agreement (JSMA) between Vertex Energy, Inc. and Bunker One (USA) Inc. aims to amend and restate the Original Co-operation Agreement, extending its term, including new businesses and territories, and addressing governance issues. Key obligations include Vertex sourcing/producing and selling the product to Bunker One, which will purchase, blend, market, sell, and deliver it in designated areas. Bunker One is also responsible for coordinating storage capacity and vessel chartering. Notable risks or penalties include Vertex's obligation to supply alternative material if they withdraw output from the JSMA, with reimbursement to Bunker One for net losses incurred. The agreement establishes a Joint Decision-Making Body and requires parties to act cooperatively to maximize profits and minimize losses.

contract_id: VirtuosoSurgicalInc_20191227_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11933379_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_License Agreement
summary: This agreement grants Virtuoso Surgical, Inc. (Company) a non-exclusive license to make, have made, import, offer for sale and sell products and services related to Active Cannulas for Bio-Sensing and Surgical Intervention from The Johns Hopkins University (JHU). In exchange, the Company must pay a license fee, cash and equity within 30 days of the Effective Date, as well as minimum annual royalties and running royalties on NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES. The Company is also responsible for providing quarterly royalty reports to JHU and making true and accurate records of sales and other information. If payments are not made when due, interest will accrue, and termination or expiration of the agreement will not affect JHU's right to recover unpaid royalties. Upon termination, all rights in and to the licensed technology will revert to JHU at no cost.

The agreement outlines the terms and conditions under which VOTOCAST provides hosting and technical services to LICENSEE. Key obligations include VOTOCAST's responsibility to protect LICENSEE and LICENSEE Users' privacy, maintain software and systems, and provide reasonable notice for scheduled maintenance or repairs. LICENSEE must offer access to the Services, obtain acceptance from each user, and pay fees in accordance with the applicable schedule. In the event of service interruption due to VOTOCAST-controlled activities, LICENSEE will receive a credit against future services. If the Services do not conform to Acceptance Criteria within 30 days, LICENSEE may terminate the agreement, but VOTOCAST is entitled to receive payment for Implementation Fees up to the date of termination.

The Exclusive Distributor Agreement between Hydraspin and Distributor appoints Distributor as the exclusive distributor of Hydraspin's products in a specific territory for a specified term. Key obligations include Distributor's responsibility to use commercially reasonable efforts to promote and sell the Products, while Hydraspin must obtain necessary authorizations and install the Products. Notable risks or penalties include the potential loss of exclusivity if Distributor fails to meet Performance Benchmarks, and Hydraspin's obligation not to contact Customers outside of Distributor without prior consent. Additionally, upon termination, each party must return confidential information and cancel outstanding orders. The agreement is governed by applicable laws in the territory and disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Collin County, Texas.

The purpose of this agreement is for Calm.com, Inc. and XpresSpa Group, Inc. to collaborate on displaying, marketing, promoting, offering for sale, and selling Calm branded products at XSPA's stores in the United States. Key obligations include Calm retaining legal title to product collateral until sold, while XSPA must use its commercially reasonable efforts to display and sell Calm branded products, prepare necessary approvals, allocate retail space, and distribute co-branded gift products. Notable risks or penalties include an exclusivity clause prohibiting XSPA from selling digital meditation or sleep products without Calm's consent, a right of first refusal for Calm to expand the agreement to new stores, and termination provisions allowing either party to terminate with written notice.
